title: Learn should skip files when block alignment is unsafe
why_this_case_exists: Git can tell us that a file changed, but learn still needs safe block pairing. When the shape changes too much, skip is the right answer.
setup: The repository is a Git repo.
The target file in HEAD and the working tree have materially different Markdown block structure.
Block index alignment is no longer trustworthy.

input.risk: Before/after blocks may refer to different content.

expected: The file is skipped for learning.
The reason is understandable from output or debug artifacts.
No new memory rule is written from this file.
should_not_happen: The tool still learns from obviously misaligned blocks.
A resulting rule mixes unrelated content from different sections.
